description: Grafana Prometheus response JSON parser never checks iter.Error from jsoniter, causing silent data loss on truncated or malformed responses

root_cause.function: Multiple parsing functions in the Prometheus response converter
root_cause.summary: The Prometheus response parser in Grafana uses json-iterator/go to parse responses. jsoniter's Iterator methods do not return errors; they set iter.Error. The converter code never checks iter.Error after parsing operations. When the response body is truncated (by dataproxy response_limit) or malformed, jsoniter stops parsing and sets iter.Error, but the converter ignores this and returns whatever partial data was extracted.
root_cause.mechanism: jsoniter iter.Error is never checked. Truncated JSON from response_limit causes jsoniter to stop mid-parse. Partial data frames are returned as if they were complete.

error_chain.action: Truncates response body if it exceeds configured limit
BUG: Parses JSON with jsoniter, never checks iter.Error, returns partial data

affected_services: Grafana Loki datasource (shares the converter code path)
Any Grafana deployment with response_limit configured
remediation: Check iter.Error after every jsoniter parsing operation in prom.go. When an error is detected (including truncation/EOF errors), return the error instead of partial data. Consider wrapping jsoniter Iterator methods to return errors directly (the PR introduces a jsonitere package for this purpose).
